message:
fix(service): make RateLimit token bucket shared across clones via Arc<Mutex>

Previously RateLimit stored its token count and last-refill timestamp as
plain fields on the struct. When a RateLimit<S> was cloned (common in
tower-style service stacks where each connection gets a clone), each clone
received an independent copy of the full token bucket. This meant N clones
could collectively issue N×rate requests per period — the rate limit was
per-handle rather than per-service, defeating its purpose under load.

The fix introduces SharedRateLimitState behind Arc<Mutex<_>> (using
parking_lot for uncontended fast-path). All clones now share a single
bucket. Reservations remain handle-local via LocalReservationState: a
clone's poll_ready grants a reservation from the shared bucket, and that
reservation is returned to the shared bucket on Drop if call() is never
invoked (preventing capacity leaks from abandoned readiness grants).

Key behavioral changes:
- Clone no longer duplicates available capacity
- Abandoned poll_ready reservations return tokens to the shared pool
- Fresh clones start with zero local reservations (cannot spend another
  handle's readiness grant)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
